  "sentiment": "positive|neutral|negative|very_negative",
  "intent_label": "greeting|feature_question|price_negotiation|walkaway|bulk_inquiry|policy_question|complaint|closing|channel_switch_request|disengage|other",
  "is_repeated_dissatisfaction": true|false,
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0
}

Definitions:
- sentiment: emotional tone of THIS message. "very_negative" = clearly angry/frustrated.
- intent_label:
  - greeting: hello, kya chahiye, hi etc.
  - feature_question: asking what the product is/has/does (size, material, charging...)
  - price_negotiation: discussing or pushing on price (counter, discount, kam karo)
  - walkaway: signalling they will leave or buy elsewhere ("aur se le lunga", "rehne do", "chodo")
  - bulk_inquiry: asking about multiple pieces / wholesale
  - policy_question: asking about return, refund, COD, exchange, delivery
  - complaint: dissatisfaction about product quality, response time, behavior
  - closing: agreeing to buy / asking payment details ("le lunga", "fix karo", "UPI?")
  - channel_switch_request: customer wants to move off Instagram —
    "WhatsApp pe baat", "call kar lo", "Instagram delete kar diya",
    "phone number do", "DM nahi karna", "email pe", "kahin aur baat"
  - disengage: customer is softly dropping off — NOT actively negotiating, NOT
    making a walk-away threat, just signalling they're done. Examples:
    "ok", "ok thanks", "bye", "ok bye", "nahi chahiye", "let me think",
    "abhi nahi", "baad mein", "thik hai bye", "rehne do" (when said calmly
    without a "kahin aur" / "aur se le lunga" follow-up).
    Critical distinction:
      - "aur se le lunga" / "kahi aur lunga" → walkaway (adversarial)
      - "rehne do bhai" / "ok" / "bye" → disengage (passive, ending chat)
  - other: anything else
- is_repeated_dissatisfaction: TRUE if the message reads like the customer is repeating a
  complaint or rejection they already expressed. Look at RECENT HISTORY for context.
- confidence: how confident you are (0.0-1.0).

Is this message asking about a specific specification or feature of the CURRENT product being discussed?
Examples of feature questions: charging method, power source, size, material, colour, weight, display type, connectivity, whether a feature exists on THIS product
NOT feature questions:
- price, warranty, delivery, return policy, 'le lunga', 'order karna hai'
- Durability / defect / 'what if it breaks' concerns, EVEN IF a feature word appears: 'kharab ho gayi toh', 'kharab nikla', 'toot gaya', 'kaam karna band ho jaye', 'led kharab ho jaye toh', 'if it stops working', 'defective nikla'. These are warranty/return concerns handled elsewhere — NOT spec questions.
- Requests to see OTHER products or designs ('koi aur design hai?', 'aur kuch dikhao', 'different model hai?', 'or sample', 'aur kya hai')
- General browsing or switching ('ye nahi, kuch aur', 'koi doosra', 'aur options')
- Compliments or reactions ('accha hai', 'sundar hai', 'theek hai')
- Expressing DISLIKE or REJECTION of a feature value — even if a tag word appears ('yeh colour nhi chahie', 'ye size nahi chalega', 'is design mein nahi chahiye', 'aur colour hai?'). These mean the customer does not want THIS product, NOT that they are asking what the feature is.
- Asking if a product EXISTS in a different variant/colour/style ('koi blue green colour type hai?', 'X mein kuch hai?', 'koi aur colour hai?', 'X colour available hai?'). These are availability/browse questions — the customer wants to see a different product, not know about THIS product's specs.
Key test: is the customer asking WHAT THIS specific product IS or HAS? Only that is a feature question. 'Do you have X colour?' or 'Is X available?' is browsing, not a feature question.

If it IS a feature question, check if it maps to one of the known tags above.

- Choose a clear, meaningful display name (e.g. 'Second Hand' for a clock seconds hand question)
- Decide value_type: 'enum' if the answer has fixed options, 'text' for free text, 'number' for measurements
- For enum: provide the most likely allowed_values list (e.g. Yes/No questions → ["Yes", "No"])
- For text/number: set allowed_values to null
